label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this question. The question is asking why weekend afternoons would become dead time without sports programs. Let me look back at the passage to find the relevant information. The passage mentions that sports events are popular on Saturday and Sunday afternoons, which are described as the slowest time periods for general TV viewing. So, during these times, normally there isn't much viewership. But sports make these times popular, especially among male viewers who might not watch TV much otherwise. The key point here is that without sports, these times would be slow or "dead time" because there's low viewership. The passage says that TV networks can sell ad time at high prices during these times because of sports. If there were no sports, those times would be dead, meaning fewer people watching. Looking at the options: A) Because there would be few viewers. That seems to align with the passage's statement about weekend afternoons being the slowest times. Without sports, viewership would drop, making it dead time. B) Advertisers off work? The passage doesn't mention advertisers' work schedules. It talks about targeting male viewers and business travelers, but not advertisers themselves being off. C) TV programs would go slowly? The term "dead time" here refers to low viewership, not the speed of the programs. D) Viewers would pay less? The passage doesn't discuss payment from viewers directly;
